rows.notes: Numbers in the rightmost three columns are clear: '13 . 3 . 2'. The description is legible but the hand is old and the word 'alin' and 'probien' are pale/abbreviated; chose the reading that matches visible letters.

rows.description: William Judge alin Richd Judge pension
rows.notes: Amounts clearly aligned as '5 . 18 . 0'. The description is partly abbreviated ('alin' and 'Richd') but consistent with neighbouring rows mentioning Judge; moderate certainty on the exact wording.
Numerals '5 . 15 . 10' are sharp and well within valid ranges. The name 'Richard Wilson' is clear; 'probien' is an abbreviation but readable.

Numbers read '9 . 18 . 8' and there is a small loop after the pence digit consistent with a halfpenny mark (interpreted as 0.5). Description letters are slanted but enough letters match 'Beaver Gardiner' and 'Burdons'.
Amount '1 . 13 . 0' is clear. Handwriting of names is more ornate and slightly faded; 'Hastley's late Freeman Arundell' is my best reading from the letter shapes.
Amounts '5 . 8 . 1' are distinct. 'Mrs Heywoods' is clearly written and unambiguous.
Amount '1 . 13 . 4' readable. Description contains abbreviations and the colon after 'B' but the family/name components align with other rows.
This is a 'Do.' style line (ditto) and the amount '0 . 6 . 0' is clearly placed in the columns; description is short and abbreviated but consistent with 'do.' usage.
Numerals '2 . 12 . 2' are distinct. Surnames are slightly stylised but readable as 'Buck' and 'Rymans' (Ryman's).

Shillings '6' is clear. There is a very small mark in the pence column resembling a farthing (quarter) sign; pence whole digit is not clear (appears blank), so I set pence_whole to 0 and fraction to 0.25. Description reads as 'Barnwells' though hand is faint.
Amount '0 . 0 . 8' is clear and isolated. Name 'Leonard' is cleanly written.
Values read '0 . 2 . 6' clearly. 'Connocks' (variant spelling) is legible.
Underlined subtotal line with numerals '46 . 17 . 6' and a small quarter/farthing mark after the pence digit (interpreted as 0.25). Alignment with column rules is clear.

Small marginal pencilled/inked adjustment line appears to read 'y 45 17 0 1/2'. The leading 'y' is unclear/contextual; numerals are faint and cramped. Interpreted the final mark as a halfpenny (0.5). Low confidence due to faintness and unusual placement.
Clear centered header in Latin 'Receipts anni praesentis 1746'. No amounts in this row; serves as a section heading.
Right column clearly shows '30 . 0 . 0'. The description reads 'Guineas Rectory half years' (hand legible).

rows.description: Hidlington in Corn Rent & Land (illegible word)
rows.notes: Amount '5 . 0 . 0' is clear. The place name looks like 'Hidlington' and the remainder 'in Corn Rent & Land...' is partly smudged/abbreviated; reading of the last word uncertain.

A narrow marginal note appears 'To 10.0 for Capons & Brown 2.15.0' (likely explanatory). No clear set of numerals aligned in the three amount columns for this line; I left amounts blank. The handwriting here is congested and partly overwritten.
Amount '4 . 0 . 0' is clear. The name 'Hadington Vicarage' is readable though the first vowel letter is stylised; context (vicarage) supports the reading.
Right-side reads '4 . 5 . 11' with a small halfpenny loop after the '11' (interpreted as 11½d). Description 'Tintinhul & Napier 1/2 year' is legible though spelling is archaic.
Amount '3 . 0 . 0' is clear. Description 'Trethavin 1/2 year' is readable.
Amount appears as '16 . 0 . 0' though the '16' has a slightly ornate '6' which made initial reading ambiguous; context of larger parish receipts supports 16 pounds.
The phrase 'Little Two Corn Rents' is visible but the amount is faint and may read '8 . 0 . 0' or possibly another number. Chose 8.0.0 based on the faint shapes; low confidence due to smudging.
Right column reads '7 . 6 . 8' fairly clearly. Name 'Taylor' is plainly written.
Amount '3 . 10 . 0' is distinct. Single-word name 'Judge' matches other occurrences on the page.
Amounts appear '3 . 8 . 8' though the last pence digit is slightly compressed; chosen reading fits the ink stroke and alignment. The name looks like 'Sivons' (or 'Simons') but spelled 'Sivons' here.
Right column clearly shows '5 . 13 . 8'. Description with initial 'B.' matches earlier Gardiner entries; 'for Burdons' is legible.
Amount '0 . 19 . 9' is readable though the zero pounds is small. The name matches earlier 'Hastley'/'Arundel' mentions though spelled slightly differently; ink is slightly faded.

This line 'Little Two Money Rents' appears with the year marker '1746' nearby and has no clear amount placed in the three rightmost columns. The text is legible; absence of aligned numerals suggests either zero or that the amount is recorded elsewhere.
Amount reads '1 . 1 . 11' (note: no fractional halfpenny visible). The parenthetical '(now Kinman)' is faint but legible; contextual cross-reference to earlier 'Mrs Heywoods' supports reading.
Right columns show '0 . 13 . 4' clearly. The description shows the same 'B:' abbreviation as earlier and '(Hesters)' in parentheses; ink is darker here and words are readable.

Final carried-up total shows clearly printed '£ 235 . 10 . 11' with a halfpenny loop after the '11' (interpreted as 11½d). The currency symbols and underline indicate this is the section total; alignment is unambiguous.
